title: alyssum trichocarpum
intro: Alyssum trichocarpum, also known as Madwort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues. Alyssum trichocarpum can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in traditional herbal preparations.
